items.title: Two-pointer — opposite ends close in
items.caption: Each step moves exactly one pointer, so the loop runs at most n times — O(n).

items.title: Sliding window — variable-size, longest sub-array with sum ≤ 10
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  S["arr: 2, 1, 4, 3, 6"]
  S --> Step1["l=0, r=0  window=[2] sum=2 valid → expand"]
  Step1 --> Step2["l=0, r=1  window=[2,1] sum=3 valid → expand"]
  Step2 --> Step3["l=0, r=2  window=[2,1,4] sum=7 valid → expand"]
  Step3 --> Step4["l=0, r=3  window=[2,1,4,3] sum=10 valid → expand"]
  Step4 --> Step5["l=0, r=4  window=[2,1,4,3,6] sum=16 INVALID → shrink"]
  Step5 --> Step6["l=1, r=4  window=[1,4,3,6] sum=14 INVALID → shrink"]
  Step6 --> Step7["l=2, r=4  window=[4,3,6] sum=13 INVALID → shrink"]
  Step7 --> Step8["l=3, r=4  window=[3,6] sum=9 valid → record len=2"]
  Step8 --> Done["max length seen = 4 (window [2,1,4,3])"]
items.caption: Each index is added once when `r` advances and removed at most once when `l` advances — total O(n).
